entries.title: 3DS SCA exemption support
entries.description: A new optional field `strong_customer_authentication_exemptions` is now available under `payment_method.detail.card.three_d_secure`. Yuno also propagates exemption recommendations from your fraud provider through the full payment route to the 3DS gateway, and the exemption type appears in the payment response, GET API, and webhooks.

entries.title: Optional first and last name for customers
entries.description: The `first_name` and `last_name` fields are now optional when creating a customer via the API. If a payment provider requires them, the SDK form will prompt the user. If you are not using the Yuno SDK, ensure these fields are provided at payment creation time when required by the provider.

entries.title: Airline trip type field
entries.description: A new `type` enum field has been added inside `additional_data.airline` to specify the trip type: `ONE_WAY`, `ROUNDTRIP`, or `MULTIPLE_DESTINATIONS`. No integration changes required if you do not use airline data.

entries.title: Airline leg ordering fields
entries.description: Two new integer fields, `order` and `route_order`, have been added inside `additional_data.airline.legs` to describe the order of legs within a route and across routes respectively. No integration changes required if you do not use airline leg data.

entries.title: Transportation data struct
entries.description: A new `transportations` array of objects is available inside `additional_data` for non-airline travel bookings such as bus, train, ship, tram, and car. Each entry supports legs, passengers, and tickets sub-objects with departure/arrival details.

entries.title: Network token enrollment for vaulted cards
entries.description: When the network tokens feature is enabled, Yuno now generates network tokens for all cards enrolled and stored in the Vault. One-time-use cards are excluded. This aligns with Visa and Mastercard network tokenization standards and improves recurring payment security and reliability.

entries.title: Subscription billing date control
entries.description: A new `billing_date` struct is available when creating a subscription, letting you define billing timing as `PREPAID`, `POSTDATE`, or a specific `DAY` of the month. This field is mutually exclusive with the `frequency` object.

entries.title: Payment method expiration date field
entries.description: A new `expirates_at` timestamp field is now returned inside `payment_method_detail.wallet` for asynchronous payment methods that support it. Availability depends on provider support.

entries.title: Customer geolocation in payments
entries.description: A new `geolocation` object with `latitude` and `longitude` fields is now available inside `customer_payer` when creating a payment.

entries.title: Cancel PENDING APM payments
entries.description: You can now cancel a `PENDING` payment created with an alternative payment method (APM) via the cancel endpoint. Previously this was restricted to card transactions only. Feature availability depends on provider support.

entries.title: Subscription webhook URL support
entries.description: You can now define a `webhook_url` in the Developers section of the dashboard to receive subscription lifecycle events. Different event types produce distinct webhook payloads.

entries.title: 3DS setup service removed
entries.description: The separate setup service call for the 3DS direct implementation is no longer required. Data collection and setup are now handled automatically inside payment creation, simplifying the integration flow.

entries.title: Extended airline leg fields
entries.description: New fields have been added inside `additional_data.airline.legs`: `arrival_airport_country`, `arrival_airport_city`, `departure_airport_country`, and `departure_airport_city`. Also, a `booking_system_code` and `booking_system_name` are now available inside `airline.tickets[].issue`. No integration changes required if you do not use airline data.

entries.title: Discounts array in order data
entries.description: A new `discounts` array of objects is available inside `additional_data.order`. Each discount entry supports `id`, `name`, and `unit_amount` fields.

entries.title: Monitors flag in routing rules
entries.description: A new boolean field `monitors` has been added inside the `routing_rules` object in the payment response, indicating whether a transaction was processed through the Monitors feature. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Customer info in refund requests
entries.description: A new `customer_payer` object is now accepted in refund requests, allowing you to pass additional customer data (e.g., `document`) to providers that require it.

entries.title: OAuth2 authentication for webhooks
entries.description: Webhooks now support OAuth2 authentication. Configure `authentication_url`, `client_secret_key`, `client_id`, and `grant_type` in the Developers/Webhooks dashboard section so Yuno can obtain tokens for secure webhook delivery.

entries.title: Dedicated URL for chargeback webhooks
entries.description: You can now configure a dedicated webhook URL specifically for chargeback events. Previously, chargebacks were bundled with the Purchase trigger. Update your webhook configuration in the dashboard to use the new dedicated chargeback URL.

entries.title: Ticket data in payment orders
entries.description: A new `tickets` array of objects is available inside `additional_data.order`, supporting fields like `id`, `name`, `description`, `type`, `amount`, and a nested `event` object with address details.

entries.title: Subscription initial payment validation
entries.description: A new boolean field `initial_payment_validation` is available when creating a subscription. Set to `true` to continue the subscription only after a successful first payment, or `false` to continue regardless of the first payment outcome.

entries.title: Card type selection for vaulted tokens
entries.description: When using a vaulted token for payment, you can now specify `card_data.type` as `CREDIT` or `DEBIT` inside `payment_method.detail.card`. Useful for markets like Brazil where the same card can be used for both transaction types.

entries.title: Account funding transaction details
entries.description: A new `account_funding` object is available inside `additional_data`, supporting `sender` and `beneficiary` sub-objects with name, email, and document fields required for Account Funding Transactions (AFT).

entries.title: Network transaction ID for stored credentials
entries.description: A new `network_transaction_id` field is now available inside `payment_method.detail.card.stored_credentials`. Recommended for subscription payments with enrolled cards to ensure traceability across the payment lifecycle.

entries.title: Smart routing flag in payment response
entries.description: A new boolean field `smart_routing` has been added inside the `routing_rules` object in the payment response, indicating whether the transaction was processed through the smart routing feature. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Trial period for subscriptions
entries.description: A new `trial_period` object is available when creating a subscription. Define `billing_cycles` and a reduced `amount` to offer partial or full (free) trial periods before full billing begins.

entries.title: Card expiration fields in transaction response
entries.description: Two new fields, `expiration_month` and `expiration_year`, are now returned inside `transactions.card_data` in the payment response. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Recipients for split marketplace payments
entries.description: A new Recipients API allows you to register recipients with multiple provider mappings before sending split payments. Use `recipient_id` in the `split_marketplace` array instead of provider-specific IDs when multiple providers are involved.

entries.title: Receipt support for refund transactions
entries.description: Refund requests now accept a `response_additional_data.receipt` boolean and an optional `receipt_language` field. When set, a receipt URL is returned in `payment.transaction.receipt_url` after the refund is processed.

entries.title: Items data for subscriptions
entries.description: A new `additional_data.order.items` array is now available when creating a subscription, allowing you to pass item-level details (category, id, name, quantity, unit_amount) to providers.

entries.title: Payouts to vaulted tokens
entries.description: The Payouts API now supports sending payouts directly to enrolled credit or debit cards using a `vaulted_token` inside `withdrawal_method`. Provider support required.

entries.title: Smart retry scheme for subscriptions
entries.description: Subscription declined transactions now support Smart Retries using machine learning to determine optimal retry timing. The retry schedule follows seven attempts over 96 hours. Configure via the Subscriptions dashboard.

entries.title: Parent payment method type field
entries.description: A new `parent_payment_method_type` field is returned inside `transactions.payment_method` indicating the wallet provider (`APPLE_PAY`, `GOOGLE_PAY`, or `CLICK_TO_PAY`) used in a direct wallet integration. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Connection ID in transaction data
entries.description: A new `connection_data.id` field is returned inside each transaction object, providing the unique identifier of the payment connection used to process the transaction. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Receipt support for cancel transactions
entries.description: Cancel requests now accept a `response_additional_data.receipt` boolean. When set, a receipt URL is returned in `payment.transaction.receipt_url` after the cancellation is processed.

entries.title: Shipping object in payment request
entries.description: A new `shipping` object is available when creating a payment, supporting fields `type`, `description`, `carrier`, and `deliver_at` to specify shipment details.

entries.title: Payment link enrollment flow
entries.description: Payment links now support card enrollment on successful payment. Set `one_time_use=true` and `vault_on_success=true` when creating a payment link to generate a new `vaulted_token` for the customer upon payment success.

links.label: Enroll cards with payment links guide

entries.title: Split marketplace payments
entries.description: A new `split_marketplace` array is now available in the payment request, allowing merchants to divide a payment among multiple recipients with configurable amounts, types (`PURCHASE`, `COMMISSION`), and liability settings. Provider support required.

entries.title: Subscription agreement ID field
entries.description: A new `subscription_agreement_id` field is available inside `payment_method.detail.card.stored_credentials`, required by certain markets and processors (e.g., Mexico) to include the agreement ID with the customer in subscription-related payments.

entries.title: Referenced payouts from transactions
entries.description: Payouts to card payment methods now support a `original_transaction_id` field inside `withdrawal_method`, enabling merchants to initiate a payout using card data from a prior transaction without needing PCI compliance.

entries.title: Currency conversion in payments
entries.description: A new `currency_conversion` object is available inside the `amount` struct, allowing you to pass `provider_currency_conversion_id`, `cardholder_currency`, and `cardholder_amount` for dual-currency settlement. Contact your technical account manager to enable.

entries.title: ISO 8583 response code and message
entries.description: Two new fields, `iso8583_response_code` and `iso8583_response_message`, are now returned inside `transactions.provider_data`, providing the direct ISO 8583 response from the provider. ABECS codes are also surfaced in these fields for Brazilian providers.

entries.title: Cancel or refund payment by payment ID
entries.description: A new endpoint allows you to cancel or refund a payment by providing only the `payment_id`, without needing the `transaction_id`. The existing transaction-level endpoint remains available.

entries.title: Wallet cryptogram for native pay
entries.description: A new `cryptogram` field is available inside `payment_method.detail.wallet` when creating a payment, enabling direct Apple Pay and Google Pay API integrations without using the Yuno SDK.

entries.title: Simplified mode for refund retries
entries.description: The `simplified_mode: true` flag is now supported in refund requests. Yuno will return a `DECLINED` status and automatically retry the refund up to five times within 24 hours, keeping the payment in `PENDING`/`RETRY_IN_PROCESS` status.

entries.title: Taxes array in order data
entries.description: A new `taxes` array of objects is available inside `additional_data.order`, supporting multiple tax lines per payment. The prior top-level `tax` object remains supported but the new array is recommended for better tax management.

entries.title: Payouts product launch
entries.description: Yuno Payouts is now available as a new API product for sending funds to beneficiaries (users, merchants, providers, customers) across banks, cards, and wallets through multiple providers. Refer to the payouts documentation to get started.

entries.title: Payment method fingerprint field
entries.description: A new `fingerprint` field is returned in the payment method object and in the payment response for card transactions. It uniquely identifies a card across multiple `vaulted_tokens` within your organization.

entries.title: Simplified mode for capture retries
entries.description: A new `simplified_mode` boolean is available in capture requests. When set to `true`, Yuno returns a `SUCCEEDED` status immediately and automatically retries failed transactions up to five times within a 24-hour period.

entries.title: Benefit type field for Pluxee payments
entries.description: A new `benefity_type` field has been added to the payment object for the Pluxee payment method in Chile. Required when using the `DIRECT` workflow.

entries.title: Routing rules in payment response
entries.description: A new `routing_rules` object is now returned in the payment response, containing a `condition` sub-object with the `id`, `name`, and `description` of the routing rule applied during payment creation. No integration changes required.

entries.title: Merchant customer created at field
entries.description: A new `merchant_customer_created_at` field (ISO 8601 format) is available in the Customer and `customer_payer` objects, representing the date the customer registered on the merchant platform.

entries.title: Installments plan in checkout session
entries.description: A new `installments.plan` array and `installments.plan_id` field are available in the checkout session object, allowing you to specify a custom installments plan per session. If omitted, the account-level plan is used.

entries.title: Callback URL for payment links
entries.description: A new `callback_url` field is available when creating a payment link, specifying the URL the customer is redirected to after completing payment.

entries.title: Transactions history array
entries.description: A new `transactions_history` array is now returned in the payment object, listing all transactions associated with a payment including those across multiple provider routes.

entries.title: Tip amount field in order data
entries.description: A new `tip_amount` field is available inside `additional_data.order`. This is informational only — the tip amount is already included in the total transaction value and is not added separately.

entries.title: In-progress capture error message
entries.description: The API now returns a specific error response when a capture is attempted while another capture is already in progress: `INVALID_STATUS` with message "Invalid transaction status. Transaction already in progress". No integration changes required.

entries.title: Neighborhood field in address objects
entries.description: A new `neighborhood` field (3–255 characters) has been added to `shipping_address` and `billing_address` in the Customer object.

entries.title: Stored credentials support
entries.description: Yuno now supports Visa and Mastercard stored credentials rules, allowing merchants to mark payments as Customer Initiated Transactions (CIT) or Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT) via the `stored_credentials` struct inside `payment_method.detail.card`.

entries.title: Provider data fields in transaction
entries.description: Three new fields are now returned inside `transactions.provider_data`: `response_code` (provider outcome code), `third_party_account_id` (processor account ID), and `third_party_transaction_id` (processor transaction ID). No integration changes required.
